name: Deva (Hinduism)
description: Deva (Sanskrit: देव, .mw-parser-output .IPA-label-small{font-size:85%}.mw-parser-output .references .IPA-label-small,.mw-parser-output .infobox .IPA-label-small,.mw-parser-output .navbox .IPA-label-small{font-size:100%}Sanskrit pronunciation: [de:vɐ]) means 'shiny', 'exalted', 'heavenly being', 'divine being', 'anything of excellence',[1] and is also one of the Sanskrit terms used to indicate a deity in Hinduism.[2] Deva is a masculine term; the feminine equivalent is Devi. The word is a cognate with Latin deus ('god') and Greek Zeus.

infobox.English: Heavenly, divine, shiny, exalted, anything of excellence, donor of knowledge or resources.
